    89→# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL
    90→
    91→## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain
    92→
    93→### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)
    94→
    95→The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.
    96→
    97→#### ECM Function Mapping
    98→
    99→| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
   100→|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
   101→| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
   102→| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
   103→| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
   104→| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |
   105→
   106→#### ECM Compliance Requirements
   107→
   108→| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
   109→|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
   110→| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
   111→| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
   112→| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
   113→| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   118→
   119→**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety
   120→
   121→EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.
   122→
   123→#### RAMS Metric Definitions
   124→
   125→| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
   126→|--------|------------|------------------|
   127→| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
   128→| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
   129→| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
   130→| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |
   131→
   132→#### RAMS Targets
   133→
   134→| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   135→|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
   136→| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
   137→| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
   138→| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
   139→| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology
   144→
   145→All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.
   146→
   147→#### Mandatory Term Usage
   148→
   149→| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   150→|---------------|------------|----------------|
   151→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   152→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
   153→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   154→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   155→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   156→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   157→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |
   158→
   159→#### Maintenance Terminology
   160→
   161→| Term | Definition | Context |
   162→|------|------------|---------|
   163→| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
   164→| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
   165→| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
   166→| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
   167→| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |
   168→

# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL

## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain

### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)

The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.

#### ECM Function Mapping

| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |

#### ECM Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |

---

### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety

EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.

#### RAMS Metric Definitions

| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
|--------|------------|------------------|
| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |

#### RAMS Targets

| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |

---

### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology

All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

#### Mandatory Term Usage

| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|---------------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |

#### Maintenance Terminology

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|------------|---------|
| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

---

## 2. Dubai Metro Context

### 2.1 Fleet Composition

| Fleet | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot | Daily Avg km |
|-------|--------|-------|------|-------|--------------|
| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya | 420-475 km/day |
| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |
| **Total** | | **128** | | | |

#### Alstom Fleet Characteristics

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Quantity | 79 trains |
| Train Designation | ALT-001 through ALT-079 |
| Cars per Train | 5 |
| Base Mileage Range | 450,000 - 800,000 km |
| Assigned Depot | Al Rashidiya |

#### Kinki Sharyo Fleet Characteristics

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Quantity | 49 trains (newer fleet) |
| Train Designation | KNK-001 through KNK-049 |
| Cars per Train | 5 |
| Base Mileage Range | 320,000 - 600,000 km |
| Assigned Depot | Jebel Ali |

   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

     4→ * Central state management for the MMS Canvas using @zazcart/tools.
     5→ * Uses useSyncExternalStore for React integration without stale closure issues.
     6→ *
     7→ * Slices:
     8→ * - viewport: Camera position, momentum, animation, panning
     9→ * - interaction: Hover and selection state for tasks
    10→ *
    11→ * @see TECH-DEBT-001
    12→ * @see F018 - Hover highlight
    13→ * @see F019 - Click to select

    16→import { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';
    17→import { viewportSlice } from './viewport';
    18→import { interactionSlice } from './interaction';

    27→    viewport: viewportSlice,
    28→    interaction: interactionSlice,
    29→  },
    30→  context: {

# Notes for Claude - Future Reference This file contains learnings, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes. --- ## Technology-Specific Notes ### React Three Fiber (R3F) #### Key Learning R3F v9 has significant API changes from v8. Always check the latest documentation. #### Common Pitfalls - Never use `useFrame` outside of Canvas context - `Html` component from drei requires `transform` prop for 3D positioning - InstancedMesh color changes require `needsUpdate = true` on the attribute #### Correct Usage ```typescript // Correct way to update InstancedMesh colors mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true; // Correct Html positioning in 3D
Content
``` --- ### Three.js Shaders (GLSL) #### Key Learning Fragment shaders for heatmaps need careful color interpolation. #### Correct Usage ```glsl // Color ramp for heatmap (Yellow → Orange → Red) vec3 ramp(float t) { vec3 a = vec3(1.0, 0.95, 0.5); // light yellow vec3 b = vec3(1.0, 0.6, 0.0); // orange vec3 c = vec3(0.85, 0.0, 0.0); // red return mix(mix(a, b, smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, t)), c, smoothstep(0.5, 1.0, t)); } ``` --- ### Bun Workspaces / Monorepo #### Key Learning Bun workspaces require proper package.json structure and cross-package imports. #### Important Configuration ```json { "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"] } ``` #### CRITICAL: Running Scripts in Monorepos **If root scripts fail, try the direct approach:** ```bash # This ALWAYS works: cd apps/canvas && bun run dev # Or check what the package is actually named: cat apps/canvas/package.json | grep '"name"' ``` #### Common Pitfalls - **Never assume root scripts work** - verify first with `./init.sh --no-server` - Root scripts use filters that must match exact package names - Always use `bun add` from the correct directory - Cross-package imports need proper exports in package.json - Run `bun i` from root after adding new packages --- ## Package/Library Notes ### @react-three/drei #### IMPORTANT: Html Component **Correct import**: `import { Html } from '@react-three/drei'` #### Why this matters The Html component bridges WebGL and DOM, allowing real HTML elements to be positioned in 3D space. #### Correct usage ```tsx
Clickable content
``` --- ### three-stdlib #### IMPORTANT: Line2 for Fat Lines **Correct import**: `import { Line2, LineGeometry, LineMaterial } from 'three-stdlib'` #### Why this matters Standard Three.js Line has 1px width limit. Line2 supports variable width. #### Correct usage ```typescript const geometry = new LineGeometry(); geometry.setPositions([x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2]); const material = new LineMaterial({ color: 0x3b82f6, linewidth: 2, resolution: new Vector2(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight) }); ``` --- ## Error Solutions ### Error: "No packages matched the filter" (Bun monorepo) **Cause**: Root script uses `--filter` with wrong package name **Solution**: 1. Check actual package name: `cat apps/canvas/package.json | grep '"name"'` 2. Run directly in the app folder: `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev` 3. Or fix the filter in root package.json to match the actual package name ### Error: "Cannot use import statement outside a module" **Cause**: Package trying to use CommonJS in ESM context **Solution**: Ensure `"type": "module"` in package.json, use proper imports ### Error: "useFrame must be called inside Canvas" **Cause**: React hook called outside R3F context **Solution**: Move component inside `` or pass state via props/context ### Error: "THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost" **Cause**: GPU memory exhausted or too many textures **Solution**: Dispose unused textures, reduce DataTexture size, check for memory leaks ### Error: InstancedMesh colors not updating **Cause**: Buffer attribute not marked for update **Solution**: Set `mesh.instanceColor!.needsUpdate = true` after changing colors --- ## General Rules 1. **Always research official documentation** before trying to resolve import/ESM errors 2. **Check for alternative versions** of packages for different bundlers 3. **Don't remove dependencies** the user explicitly requested - find the correct way to use them 4. **Test after every change** - don't batch multiple changes 5. **Commit frequently** - small, focused commits are better than large ones 6. **Use bun add** - never manually edit package.json for dependencies --- ## Project-Specific Gotchas ### LOD State Machine The LOD (Level of Detail) is controlled by zoom level: - `zoom < 0.5` → 'macro' (shader heatmap) - `0.5 ≤ zoom ≤ 2.0` → 'meso' (InstancedMesh) - `zoom > 2.0` → 'micro' (HTML overlay) ### Virtual Scroll Sync Sidebar scroll is synced with camera Y using: ```typescript const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24; // DOM → Camera const y = -el.scrollTop / PX_PER_TRAIN; // Camera → DOM el.scrollTop = -viewport.y * PX_PER_TRAIN; ``` ### Spatial Hash Grid MesoLayer uses spatial hashing for O(1) instance picking: - Grid cell size matches task dimensions - Hash key: `${Math.floor(x / cellSize)}_${Math.floor(y / cellSize)}` --- ## Useful Commands ```bash # Install all workspace dependencies bun i # Start development server bun run dev # Type check all packages bun run typecheck # Build for production bun run build # Check git status git status # View recent commits git --no-pager log --oneline -10 ``` --- ## Resources - [React Three Fiber Docs](https://docs.pmnd.rs/react-three-fiber) - [Drei Docs](https://github.com/pmndrs/drei) - [Three.js Docs](https://threejs.org/docs/) - [BlueprintJS Docs](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/) - [Bun Docs](https://bun.sh/docs) - [Vite Docs](https://vitejs.dev/guide/)

# @mms/canvas

Production application for the Metro Integrated Planning System - a high-performance "Semantic Zoom" visualization for railway maintenance planning.

## Quick Start

```bash
# From this directory
bun run dev

# Or from root
cd ../.. && bun run dev --filter=@mms/canvas
```

The app runs at http://localhost:5173

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Version | Purpose |
|------------|---------|---------|
| **Vite** | 5.x | Build tool and dev server |
| **React** | 18.3.1 | UI framework (required for BlueprintJS 6) |
| **BlueprintJS** | 6.1.0 | UI component library (Palantir) |
| **R3F** | 8.x | React Three Fiber for WebGL |
| **drei** | 9.x | R3F helpers and components |
| **Three.js** | 0.170.x | 3D rendering engine |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── main.tsx              # Entry point
├── App.tsx               # Main component with layout
├── components/           # BlueprintJS UI components
│   ├── AppLayout.tsx         # Navbar, Sidebar, Canvas container
│   ├── TrainSidebar.tsx      # Train IDs (Card)
│   ├── ZoomControls.tsx      # ButtonGroup +/-
│   └── LODIndicator.tsx      # Tag component
├── canvas/               # R3F rendering
│   ├── InfiniteCanvas.tsx
│   ├── CameraController.tsx
│   └── layers/
│       ├── MacroLayer.tsx    # Heatmap (InstancedMesh/Shader)
│       ├── MesoLayer.tsx     # Task blocks (InstancedMesh)
│       └── MicroLayer.tsx    # Detailed cards (Html)
├── data/
│   ├── types.ts              # Train, Task interfaces
│   └── generator.ts          # Mock data generation
└── hooks/
    └── useViewport.ts        # zoom, pan, LOD state
```

## LOD System (Level of Detail)

The canvas implements three rendering layers based on camera zoom:

| LOD | Zoom | Visualization | Technical |
|-----|------|---------------|-----------|
| **Macro** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (yellow→orange→red) | InstancedMesh or Shader |
| **Meso** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (DNA pattern) | InstancedMesh |
| **Micro** | > 2.0x | Detailed task cards | drei Html + CSS |

## Features (see features.json)

- **F101**: Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS skeleton ✅
- **F102-F103**: R3F Canvas + Camera setup
- **F104-F107**: Data, MesoLayer, pan/zoom, LOD
- **F108-F110**: MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions
- **F111-F114**: Sidebar, timeline, controls
- **F115-F117**: Instructions, performance, visual parity

## Commands

```bash
bun run dev        # Start dev server
bun run build      # Production build
bun run preview    # Preview production build
bun run typecheck  # Type check
```

## Visual Reference

See `../../design/` for pixel-perfect target specifications.

## Related

- **POC Reference**: `../../design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html` - Visual reference only
- **Domain Docs**: `../../docs/DOMAIN.md` - Fleet, maintenance types
- **UX Spec**: `../../docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md` - Interaction patterns

The project is in an advanced state with most core features implemented, using modern tooling (Vite, Bun, TypeScript, @zazcart/tools state management). **Current Status**: V1.0 Genesis largely complete. 6 features remaining (mostly accessibility/keyboard navigation). ~150KB of documentation across 18 markdown files covering domain, architecture, UX, performance, and specifications. --- ## 1. APPS/CANVAS - CURRENT STATE ### 2.1 Core Architecture **Tech Stack:** - **React 18.3.1** + **Vite 5.x** (build/dev) - **React Three Fiber 8.17.10** + **Three.js 0.170.0** (WebGL) - **drei 9.114.3** (R3F helpers: Html, Line, etc.) - **BlueprintJS 6.1.0** (UI components: Navbar, Card, Button) - **@zazcart/tools 0.1.0** (State machine library) - **rbush 4.0.1** (R-tree spatial indexing) - **D3 modules** (d3-array, d3-scale, d3-time, d3-scale-chromatic, d3-zoom, etc.) ### 2.2 Application Structure **Entry Point**: `App.tsx` (395 lines) - Renders navbar with zoom controls - Sidebar with 50 trains (clickable navigation) - TimelineHeader (date navigation) - InfiniteCanvas (R3F rendering) - Minimap (bird's eye view) - TaskTooltip (hover info) - TaskDetailsPanel (selection) - PerformancePanel (FPS stats) **Canvas Rendering**: `InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (2106 lines - VERY LARGE) - OrthographicCamera setup - Pan/zoom interaction handling - LOD (Level of Detail) system with smooth transitions - Three layers: MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer - Viewport culling with RBush spatial index - Momentum/inertial panning - Animation state machine **Layers**: - **MacroLayer** (Heatmap): Visible at zoom < 0.5. Uses InstancedMesh + d3-scale-chromatic for yellow→orange→red gradient - **MesoLayer** (Task Blocks): Visible at zoom 0.5-2.0. InstancedMesh for all task blocks with conflict indicators - **MicroLayer** (Detailed Cards): Visible at zoom > 2.0. HTML overlays with task details, grid lines, labels **State Management**: `state/` - `canvas-machine.ts`: Creates state machine with two slices (viewport, interaction) - `viewport.ts`: Camera position, momentum, animation, canvas size - `interaction.ts`: Hover/selection/focus state for tasks - Uses `@zazcart/tools` with `metadata: false` for 60fps optimization ### 2.3 Data & Types **Mock Data Generator** (`data/generator.ts`): - 50 trains (30 ALSTOM, 20 KINKI Sharyo) - 10 years of tasks per train - ~8-12 tasks per train per year - ~5% have conflict flags - Generated on-demand (no persistence) **Domain Types** (`data/types.ts`): - `Train`: id, name, assetType - `Task`: id, trainId, startDate, endDate, type, hasConflict, predecessorId - `TaskRender`: Computed positions (trainIndex, startDay, duration, color) - `WorldBounds`: Navigation limits - `ViewConfig`: Pixel scales (pxPerDay=10, pxPerTrain=24) --- ## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION (@zazcart/tools) ### 3.1 Usage Pattern ```typescript // canvas-machine.ts export const canvasMachine = createMachine({ id: 'canvas', slices: { viewport: viewportSlice, // Camera, momentum, animation interaction: interactionSlice, // Hover, selection, focus }, metadata: false, // ⚠️ CRITICAL: Disables metadata tracking for 60fps }); // Component access const useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine; const useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector; const canvasStore = { getState, dispatch, subscribe }; ``` ### 3.2 Viewport Slice (viewport.ts) **State**: - `camera`: { x, y } - world coordinates - `canvasSize`: { width, height } - `isInitialized`: boolean - `momentum`: { velocityX, velocityY, isActive } - `animation`: { isAnimating, startTime, duration, startX/Y/Zoom, targetX/Y/Zoom } - `isPanning`: boolean - Pan start/camera positions **Reducers**: - `setCamera(pos)`, `setCameraX(x)`, `setCameraY(y)` - `setCanvasSize(size)` - `initialize({ camera, canvasSize })` - `startMomentum`, `updateMomentum`, `stopMomentum` - `startAnimation`, `updateAnimation`, `stopAnimation` - `startPan`, `updatePan`, `endPan` - `reset()` ### 3.3 Interaction Slice (interaction.ts) **State**: - `hoveredTaskId`: string | null (F018) - `mouseWorldPos`: { x, y } | null - `selectedTaskId`: string | null (F019) - `focusedTaskId`: string | null (F021 - keyboard navigation) - `isHoverable`: boolean **Reducers**: - `setHoveredTask(id)`, `clearHover()` - `setSelectedTask(id)`, `clearSelection()` - `setFocusedTask(id)`, `clearFocus()` - `setMouseWorldPos(pos)` - `reset()` --- ## 4. CANVAS/R3F IMPLEMENTATION ### 4.1 LOD System **Three-Layer Architecture** with smooth blending: | LOD | Zoom | Render Method | Visibility | |-----|------|---------------|-----------| | **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (InstancedMesh) | Strategic overview - monthly cells | | **MESO** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (InstancedMesh) | Default view - DNA pattern | | **MICRO** | > 2.0x | Detail cards (Html) | Detailed inspection - labels, grid | **Blend Zones** (±0.15 around thresholds): - MACRO → MESO at 0.35-0.65 zoom - MESO → MICRO at 1.85-2.15 zoom - Smooth opacity transitions between layers **Implementation**: - `calculateLayerOpacities(zoom)` → { macro, meso, micro } opacities - `LODLayers` component renders all visible layers with interpolated opacity - Each layer controls its own visibility/culling ### 4.2 Performance Optimizations 1. **InstancedMesh Pre-allocation** (PERF-001): - Single draw call per layer (instead of 1000s) - Pre-allocate 100k instances max - Use `mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` for dynamic updates 2. **Viewport Culling** (PERF-002): - RBushSpatialIndex for O(log n) queries - `calculateViewportBounds()` expands render viewport slightly - MicroLayer: `filterVisibleTasks()` reduces HTML elements - Limit to 50 HTML labels in MicroLayer 3. **Object Pooling**: - Reuse Object3D temp objects in loops - Avoid allocations in animation loops 4. **GC Avoidance**: - `@zazcart/tools` configured with `metadata: false` - Prevents object allocations on every dispatch - Direct state mutations (Immer-based) ### 4.3 Interaction Handling **Pan**: - Mouse drag updates camera position - Bounds-checked to world limits - Momentum physics option (currently disabled - BUG-008) **Zoom**: - Scroll wheel (Google Maps-style) - Trackpad pinch support (with workarounds for BUG-009) - Zoom centered on cursor position - Smooth animation (250ms) vs direct application (trackpad) **Gestures**: - Keyboard: Arrow keys for pan, +/- for zoom (F027, F028) - Click: Select tasks (F019) - Hover: Highlight tasks + tooltip (F018, F020) - Keyboard focus: Navigate tasks (F021) --- ## 5. BLUEPRINTJS USAGE PATTERNS ### 5.1 Components Used ```tsx // Navbar with groups MMS Canvas MESO
